title: Frontend clarity pass for first successful dictation
summary: Frontend-only implementation improved first-successful-dictation clarity by adding readiness messaging in auth, a clearer first-launch hint, and a small first-run onboarding sentence.
lessons: Night Shift correctly prioritized first successful dictation.
Codex implemented a safe frontend-only clarity improvement.
Future UI clarity tasks should prefer frontend-only scope before backend/auth/Rust changes.
Night Shift initially proposed too many sensitive files; future task generation should narrow scope.

title: Fix: First successful dictation — activation_failed retry state
summary: V6 handoff loop generated a scoped implementation task from an approved product proposal. Implementation added a visible fallback error message and an active retry button for the activation_failed state. Out-of-scope formatting changes were cleaned before commit. All hooks passed: Prettier, ESLint, translation check.
tests_reported: manual test pending — all 5 activation states
lessons: V6 handoff successfully converted a product patch proposal into a scoped implementation task.
Product implementation must be checked for formatting-only out-of-scope changes before commit.
For activation UI fixes, frontend-only AuthPortal.tsx changes can be enough when useAuthFlow already exposes the required callback/state.
Handoff scope rules worked: no backend, no auth client, no license client, no Rust.

title: V12 Experiment 1 — Windows paste restore delay floor 450ms → 150ms
summary: Reduced Windows clipboard restore delay floor from 450ms to 150ms (clipboard.rs:120). Founder smoke tests passed: Notepad, VS Code, Chrome/Gmail — no paste failure, no clipboard restore failure. Full 21-case matrix (Slack, Teams, Word still pending). No post-fix paste_latency_ms benchmarks recorded yet.
tests_reported: formal paste_latency_ms benchmark: pending (≥5 observations needed)
lessons: 150ms restore floor passed native and browser apps on first smoke test.
Electron apps (Slack, Teams) require dedicated testing — not yet confirmed.
V12 improve loop (propose → gate → implement → test → measure → compare → learn) executed successfully for Phase 1–4.
One-line Rust change requires cargo check + manual paste validation, not just compile — OS timing behaviour cannot be unit-tested.
